note: Minimal launch trigger set. Expand to 20 cases (10 should-trigger + 10 should-not) after first real session; flag for skill-evolution at 3+ missed activations.

should_trigger.query: Challenge my assumptions on the CMS 2026 prior-auth final rule impact
Let's go back to first principles on this vendor consolidation decision
What are we really solving here?
Why do we believe our member retention strategy is the right one?
I want to rethink this from scratch — forget what we've done before
Is this actually the right question, or are we solving the wrong problem?
Can you stress-test this recommendation with a pre-mortem?
[Advisor] Strategic-deep research on healthcare M&A fit — big decision, board-level, multi-year
Help me think through build-vs-buy for this — I want to make sure we're not anchored to 'build'
We keep saying 'it has to be this way' — let's test that

should_not_trigger.reason: single-file bug fix; not a strategic decision

should_not_trigger.query: Generate a deck from this research report
What's the syntax for a Salesforce trigger?
Write the executive summary for this research

should_not_trigger.reason: definition lookup; skill activates when applying the framework to a decision, not when defining it

should_not_trigger.query: Summarize what first-principles thinking is in one paragraph
should_not_trigger.reason: explainer request; doesn't need the full 4-phase pipeline

should_not_trigger.query: Our production is down — how do we recover?
should_not_trigger.reason: urgent hotfix; skill should DECLINE with redirect (part of the decline test)

should_not_trigger.query: Rename this variable across the codebase
should_not_trigger.reason: pure refactor; no assumptions to challenge
